title: Contributing to the gsm Universe
text: Thank interest contributing gsm suite packages! repository follows centralized gsm Contributor Guidelines maintained gsm.core. Please review guidelines opening issues submitting pull requests.

title: Contributing to the gsm Universe
text: File issue opening pull request. Use appropriate issue template. Track issue gsm Roadmap Project Board. code changes made branch submitted PR following guidelines linked . detailed workflows (branching, releases, QC, CI/CD), please see full Contributor Guidelines gsm.core.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Rogoza R, Maxwell L, Zhu Z (2026). gsm.datasim: Synthetic Test Data Generator. R package version 1.1.2, https://github.com/Gilead-BioStats/gsm.datasim.
code: @Manual{,   title = {gsm.datasim: Synthetic Test Data Generator},   author = {Roman Rogoza and Laura Maxwell and Zelos Zhu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.1.2},   url = {https://github.com/Gilead-BioStats/gsm.datasim}, }

title: Synthetic Test Data Generator
text: gsm.datasim package provides functionality produce sample data use testing development applications packages related Risk-Based Quality Monitoring (RBQM) Clinical Trials. Given inputs regarding synthetic Study’s desired ID, Participant Count, Site Count, Snapshot Count, Key Risk Indicators constructed, package generates data pertaining : Participant demographics Adverse effects Trial discontinuation Lab results Visit information Study site location /E criteria Study metadata Default, package constructs data create KRIs available {gsm} package.

what: Installation *Temporarily broken due to repo permissions issue*
title: Synthetic Test Data Generator
text: can install development version gsm.datasim GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Gilead-BioStats/gsm.datasim@dev")

title: Synthetic Test Data Generator
text: See inst/examples/run_gsm_workflows.R script run gsm workflows using data generated raw_data_generator. Result analysis reporting data along html report outputs.
code: # Generate raw data using specified parameters  data <- raw_data_generator(ParticipantCount = 100, SiteCount = 10, StudyID = "Study01", SnapshotCount = 5)   # Generate raw data using specified parameters and Mappings  data <- raw_data_generator(ParticipantCount = 100,                             SiteCount = 10,                             StudyID = "Study01",                             SnapshotCount = 5,                             workflow_path = "workflow/1_mappings",                             mappings = c("Raw_STUDY", "Raw_SITE", "Raw_SUBJ", "Raw_ENROLL",)   # Generate raw data using a template file (currently it runs on a smaller template but not always on a larger one),  # this is our current goto sim process:  data <- raw_data_generator(template_path = "~/gsm.datasim/data-raw/small_template.csv")

what: Generate Longitudinal Clinical Trial Data with Integrated Workflow — create_longitudinal_study
title: Generate Longitudinal Clinical Trial Data with Integrated Workflow — create_longitudinal_study
text: Creates longitudinal study multiple time points optional integrated pipeline processing. function provides clean, intuitive interface generating realistic clinical trial data time.

title: Generate Longitudinal Clinical Trial Data with Integrated Workflow — create_longitudinal_study
code: create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "STUDY-001",   participants = 100,   sites = 10,   timepoints = 5,   interval = "1 month",   start_date = Sys.Date() - 365,   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT"),   include_pipeline = FALSE )

title: Generate Longitudinal Clinical Trial Data with Integrated Workflow — create_longitudinal_study
text: study_id Study identifier participants Number study participants sites Number study sites timepoints Number data collection timepoints interval Time interval collection points (e.g., "1 month", "2 weeks") start_date Study start date domains Clinical domains include (e.g., c("AE", "LB", "VISIT")) include_pipeline Whether run full analytics pipeline (raw -> mapped -> metrics -> reports)

title: Generate Longitudinal Clinical Trial Data with Integrated Workflow — create_longitudinal_study
text: LongitudinalStudy object containing generated data results

title: Generate Longitudinal Clinical Trial Data with Integrated Workflow — create_longitudinal_study
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple longitudinal study study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "ONCOLOGY-001",   participants = 200,   sites = 15,   timepoints = 6,   interval = "2 months",   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT") )  # With full analytics pipeline study_with_analytics <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "CARDIO-002",    participants = 500,   sites = 25,   timepoints = 12,   interval = "1 month",   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT", "QUERY"),   include_pipeline = TRUE )  # Access results intuitively raw_data <- study_with_analytics$raw_data site_analytics <- study_with_analytics$analytics$by_site country_analytics <- study_with_analytics$analytics$by_country } # }

what: Generate Complete Longitudinal Study with Full Pipeline Processing — generate_complete_longitudinal_study
title: Generate Complete Longitudinal Study with Full Pipeline Processing — generate_complete_longitudinal_study
text: function directly addresses workflow shown issue #95 providing single function call replace complex manual orchestration multiple packages workflow steps.

title: Generate Complete Longitudinal Study with Full Pipeline Processing — generate_complete_longitudinal_study
code: generate_complete_longitudinal_study(   participant_count = 1000,   site_count = 150,   study_id = "AA-AA-000-0000",   snapshot_count = 3,   snapshot_width = "months",   start_date = as.Date("2025-02-01"),   core_mappings = c("AE", "COUNTRY", "DATACHG", "DATAENT", "ENROLL", "LB", "VISIT", "PD",     "PK", "QUERY", "STUDY", "STUDCOMP", "SDRGCOMP", "SITE", "SUBJ", "IE", "EXCLUSION") )

title: Generate Complete Longitudinal Study with Full Pipeline Processing — generate_complete_longitudinal_study
text: participant_count Number study participants (default: 1000) site_count Number study sites (default: 150) study_id Study identifier (default: "AA-AA-000-0000") snapshot_count Number time points (default: 3) snapshot_width Time snapshots (default: "months") start_date Study start date core_mappings Vector core mapping types include

title: Generate Complete Longitudinal Study with Full Pipeline Processing — generate_complete_longitudinal_study
text: List containing pipeline results equivalent manual workflow

title: Generate Complete Longitudinal Study with Full Pipeline Processing — generate_complete_longitudinal_study
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Direct replacement for the manual workflow in issue #95 results <- generate_complete_longitudinal_study(   participant_count = 1000,   site_count = 150,   study_id = "AA-AA-000-0000",   snapshot_count = 3,   snapshot_width = "months" )  # Access results similar to manual workflow site_results <- results$reporting_site country_results <- results$reporting_country study_results <- results$reporting_study } # }

what: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
title: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
text: function generates list study data snapshots based provided specifications, including participant count, site count, study ID, number snapshots generated. data simulated series months, key variables subject enrollment, adverse events, time study populated according study's specifications.

title: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
code: generate_rawdata_for_single_study(   SnapshotCount,   SnapshotWidth,   ParticipantCount,   SiteCount,   StudyID,   workflow_path,   mappings,   package,   strStartDate = "2012-01-01",   desired_specs = NULL )

title: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
text: SnapshotCount integer specifying number snapshots generate. SnapshotWidth character specifying frequency snapshots, defaults "months". Accepts "days", "weeks", "months" "years". User can also place number unit "3 months". ParticipantCount integer specifying number participants study. SiteCount integer specifying number sites study. StudyID string specifying study identifier. workflow_path string specifying path workflow mappings. mappings string specifying names workflows run. package string specifying package workflows used MakeWorkflowList() located. strStartDate string denote first snapshot simulated data occurs desired_specs list specifications data types included.

title: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
text: list data snapshots, element contains simulated data particular snapshot period (typically month), variables populated according provided specifications.

title: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
text: function generates snapshots sequence months, starting "2012-01-01". snapshot: number adverse events (ae_num) simulated. number participants screened determined. Key variables (subject ID, enrollment date, time study, etc.) populated data type specified combined_specs.

title: Generate Snapshots for Single Study Data — generate_rawdata_for_single_study
code: snapshots <- generate_rawdata_for_single_study(   SnapshotCount = 3,   SnapshotWidth = "months",   ParticipantCount = 50,   SiteCount = 5,   StudyID = "ABC",   workflow_path = what: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
text: function generates raw data study snapshots based provided participant count, site count, study ID, snapshot count. parameters NULL, loads template CSV file iterate multiple study configurations. generated raw data saved RDS file.

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
code: raw_data_generator(   ParticipantCount = NULL,   SiteCount = NULL,   StudyID = NULL,   SnapshotCount = NULL,   SnapshotWidth = NULL,   template_path = system.file("template.csv", package = "gsm.datasim"),   workflow_path = "workflow/1_mappings",   generate_reports = FALSE,   mappings = NULL,   package = "gsm.mapping",   strStartDate = "2012-01-01",   save = FALSE )

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
text: ParticipantCount integer specifying number participants study. NULL, function use values template file. SiteCount integer specifying number sites study. NULL, function use values template file. StudyID string specifying study identifier. NULL, function use values template file. SnapshotCount integer specifying number snapshots study. NULL, function use values template file. SnapshotWidth character specifying frequency snapshots, defaults "months". Accepts "days", "weeks", "months" "years". User can also place number unit "3 months". template_path string specifying path template CSV file. Default "~/gsm.datasim/inst/template.csv". workflow_path string specifying path workflow mappings. Default "workflow/1_mappings". generate_reports boolean, specifying whether produce reports upon execution. Default FALSE. mappings string specifying names workflows run. package string specifying package workflows used MakeWorkflowList() located. Default "gsm". strStartDate string denote first snapshot simulated data occurs save boolean, specifying whether saved RDS

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
text: list raw data generated study snapshot, saved RDS file "data-raw/raw_data.RDS".

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
text: function performs following steps: ParticipantCount, SiteCount, StudyID, SnapshotCount NULL, function reads template.csv file get necessary parameters multiple studies. generates raw data study snapshots based either provided parameters template file. generated data saved RDS file returned list.

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — raw_data_generator
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Generate raw data using specified parameters data <- raw_data_generator(ParticipantCount = 100, SiteCount = 10, StudyID = "Study01", SnapshotCount = 5, SnapshotWidth = "months")   # Generate raw data using a template file data <- raw_data_generator() } # }

what: Example of fluent interface usage — study_builder_examples
title: Example of fluent interface usage — study_builder_examples
text: Example fluent interface usage

title: Example of fluent interface usage — study_builder_examples
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple study with standard datasets study_data <- create_study("ONCOLOGY001") %>%   with_study_design(participants = 200, sites = 15) %>%   over_time(start_date = "2023-01-01", snapshots = 12, frequency = "months") %>%   with_standard_datasets(adverse_events = TRUE, visits = TRUE) %>%   with_adverse_events(rate_per_patient = 2.5, temporal_pattern = "increasing") %>%   generate()    # Preview configuration before generation create_study("TRIAL002") %>%   with_study_design(participants = 100, sites = 10) %>%   with_standard_datasets() %>%   preview()    # Custom dataset configuration study_data <- create_study("CUSTOM001") %>%   with_study_design(participants = 300, sites = 20) %>%   add_custom_dataset("Raw_Biomarker",                      count = function(config) config$study_params$participant_count * 5,                     depends_on = "Raw_SUBJ") %>%   generate() } # }

title: gsm.datasim v1.1.2
text: release resolves bug involving duplicate records randomization domain.

title: gsm.datasim v1.1.1
text: release adds new contributor guidelines standardized issue templates.

title: gsm.datasim v1.1.0
text: New Mapping Workflows Support: - Additional workflow support “IE” domain - dates/timestamp support many domains now available.

title: gsm.datasim v1.0.0
text: excited announce first major release gsm.datasim package, collection functions generate synthetic test data RBQM Clinical Trials based several parameters.

title: gsm.datasim v1.0.0
text: User-facing functions - generate_rawdata_for_single_study() can create snapshot(s) single study provided proper parameters appropriate mapping specifications. - raw_data_generator() wrapper run generate_rawdata_for_single_study() create multiple studies providing template/dataset containing variety parameters. New Mapping Workflows Support: - workflows exist gsm.mapping’s inst/workflow/1_mappings now supported, “STUDY”, “SUBJ”, “SITE” “ENROLL” always required. - many gsm.endpoints specific domains, Anticancer OverallResponse now support well. Replacing clindata gsm.datasim: - object gsm.core::lSource created using gsm.datasim examples, tests, vignettes across gsm ecosystem. object based “core mappings” include: “AE”, “COUNTRY”, “DATACHG”, “DATAENT”, “ENROLL”, “LB”, “PD”, “PK”, “QUERY”, “STUDY”, “STUDCOMP”, “SDRGCOMP”, “SITE”, “SUBJ”
